basics.name: Sebestyén Kamp
basics.label: Biomedical AI Researcher
basics.summary: PhD candidate in Biomedical AI at the University of Edinburgh, researching graph neural networks for biomedical informatics. Head of Research at Garandor. Former Data Team Lead at Turbine.AI.

work.name: Garandor
work.summary: Directing R&D of invisible watermarking algorithms to authenticate digital content and provenance research. Building and mentoring a multidisciplinary team. Leading technical partnerships aligned with the EU AI Act.

work.name: Turbine.AI
work.summary: Managed a team of data scientists, bioinformaticians, and chemoinformaticians for processing biological data and developing machine learning tools. Led drug discovery projects in collaboration with pharmaceutical partners including Bayer.

work.name: Turbine.AI
work.summary: Designed internal RNA-Seq analysis workflows. Developed interactive analytic and visualisation tools in R (Shiny) and Python (Dash).

work.name: Turbine.AI
work.summary: Integrated compound data from public and proprietary databases. Developed custom workflows for transcriptomic and genomic data processing.

courses: Graph Neural Networks for Biomedical Informatics

education.institution: Pázmány Péter Catholic University & Semmelweis University
Pázmány Péter Catholic University & Semmelweis University

publications.name: GNN-Suite: a Graph Neural Network Benchmarking Framework for Biomedical Informatics
publications.summary: A benchmarking framework for evaluating graph neural networks on biomedical informatics tasks.

publications.name: Network-driven cancer cell avatars for combination discovery and biomarker identification for DNA damage response inhibitors
publications.summary: Using network-driven cancer cell avatars for drug combination discovery and biomarker identification.
